texto: When animals do something noble we say they are behaving ‘like humans’. When humans do something disgusting we say they are behaving ‘like animals’. Clumsy use of the English language perpetuates the myth that animals are inferior and disposable beings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Phillip Wollen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “When animals do something noble we say they are behaving ‘like humans’. When humans do something …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, animals, humans pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humans e language.
